claims.statement: A quantity equation expresses a relationship among quantities and is independent of the units chosen to express those quantities.
claims.scope: Symbolic quantity equations in introductory quantitative science.
claims.uncertainty: Unit independence does not establish that the selected equation is the correct model for a system.

claims.statement: An equation among numerical values depends on the units in which the associated quantities are expressed.
claims.scope: Numerical-value equations tied to explicitly stated compatible units.
claims.uncertainty: The lesson does not teach unit conversion or measurement uncertainty.

claims.statement: Algebraically equivalent rearrangements of a valid quantity equation retain the same quantity relationship when operations are defined and restrictions such as nonzero divisors are preserved.
claims.scope: Equality-preserving elementary algebra over real-valued quantities with declared domains.
claims.uncertainty: Nonreversible operations can introduce or remove candidate solutions and require additional checking.
